Continuation training on the co-trained Question/Answer pairs. After the off-by-one fix, post-fix measurement showed plain finetuning scoring slightly below the base model on every axis, so the configured approach is now consolidated to one option: pretraining-data replay at half the micro-batches plus validation early stopping. The variant-sweep harness remains available.

Stage four, a pipeline default running one job per rung. The pretrained model continues training on the pairs with response-only next-token loss, drawing replay_fraction of micro-batches from the packed pretraining data and stopping on held-out pair loss. Its earlier catastrophic collapse was a target-alignment bug (node 30), now fixed; the residual measured effect of plain finetuning was mild degradation, which replay and early stopping address.

History in brief: the original role-token design collapsed tiny models; the co-training redesign kept a separate stage that still collapsed; the collapse was root-caused to an off-by-one in the target construction (a copy objective) and fixed (node 30). The first post-fix full-rung measurement on the broadened corpus and updated evaluation showed the base pretrained model at grammar 5.62 and coherence 4.84 against the sft baseline at 4.44 and 3.72, with factual accuracy about 1.4 for both: finetuning was no longer destructive, but plain finetuning was mildly lossy, consistent with residual forgetting, and the dominant failure (semantic drift, unstable referent binding) was shared with the base model, that is, a pretraining-scale property rather than an SFT problem. The decision was then to focus on one SFT option rather than sweep five every run: the default finetune configuration combines replay_fraction 0.5 with validation_fraction 0.05 and early_stop_patience 3, and finetune.variants is empty, so each rung runs a single finetune job. The sweep harness (node 44) stays in the code for future comparisons by listing variants.
